_meta.purpose: Phase 1 of the Voting Record Matrix: maps every row of the Maine Tax Tracker (the 32 increases in `html`) to the legislative roll call that enacted it, with the legislature, session, and date.
_meta.why_bills_not_taxes: Legislators vote on bills, not on individual taxes. Most of the 32 tracker rows were bundled into a handful of omnibus budget bills, so many rows share ONE roll call. This file is keyed by bill; each bill lists the tracker rows it enacted.

verification_key.reported: Vote tally reported by press; confirm exact roll-call number against the official Maine Legislature journal before publishing member-level claims.
verification_key.date-confirmed: Bill identity and enactment date confirmed; exact yea/nay tally still needs the official journal.
verification_key.needs-lookup: LD number and/or tally not yet pinned down; requires the official Maine Legislature record.
verification_key.attribution-risk: No clean member-level roll call on the tax provisions exists (voice vote / 'under the hammer' / administrative action). Do NOT assert individual votes without resolving this.
verification_key.no-roll-call: Not a legislative vote at all.

_meta.sources_note: The authoritative record is the Maine Legislature roll-call system (legislature.maine.gov roll calls + LawMakerWeb) and the enacted-law PDFs at lldc.mainelegislature.org. Those pages block automated fetching, so tallies marked date-confirmed/needs-lookup must be read there by hand before member-level publication.

bills.title: FY2026–27 biennial budget — change package

roll_calls.note: party-line: all Democrats yea, all Republicans nay

bills.note: Two-step budget. The majority 'Part 1' continuing-services budget (PL 2025, c. 2, March 2025) carried no tax increases; every tax/fee increase lives in THIS change package. Non-emergency, so it passed by simple majority and takes effect 90 days after adjournment.

bills.title: 2026 supplemental budget
bills.note: Earlier 'under the hammer' concern is RESOLVED: there is a recorded member-level enactment roll call in each chamber — House RC #791 (76–71, Apr 8 2026) and Senate RC #927 (18–16, Apr 9 2026); signed Apr 10. Verified against the official roll-call records now stored in sources/rollcalls/. These are the votes attributed for rows 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 29, 32 in the voting matrix.

bills.title: FY2024–25 biennial budget — change package
bills.note: Row 10 = the Paid Family & Medical Leave program (and its 1% payroll tax), created inside this budget. Rows 22 (PTDZ/ETIF/MCIC sunset → Dirigo) and 24 (senior property-tax freeze repeal) are also here. The March 2023 majority 'Part 1' budget was PL 2023, c. 2.

bills.title: 2024 supplemental budget
bills.note: LD 2214 ↔ c. 643 to be confirmed against the chaptered law. Rows 3 (canned software) & 7 (lease-stream timing) were ALSO enacted via an identical standalone bill, PL 2023, c. 673 (see below) — same policy, separate vote.

bills.title: Canned software / electronically transferred products made taxable (standalone)

roll_calls.note: LD number and tally need lookup
LD number and tally need lookup

bills.note: Identical companion to the c. 643 provisions for rows 3 & 7. For the matrix, pick ONE vote to attribute (recommend the supplemental-budget vote, c. 643 / LD 2214) and footnote the companion to avoid double-counting.

bills.title: "Tobacco products" definition expanded to include synthetic/artificial nicotine

roll_calls.note: LD number and tally need lookup
LD number and tally need lookup

bills.title: 2019 tax conformity act — Maine Capital Investment Credit cut (9% → 1.2%)
bills.note: Row 19 has TWO enacting laws. The MCIC cut is here (LD 1671 / c. 527). The FDII add-back is in PL 2021, c. 1 (see below).

bills.title: FY2021 supplemental / COVID tax-conformity act
bills.note: Row 18 = decoupling from CARES Act business provisions. Row 19 (FDII add-back portion) is also here. Likely an emergency measure (2/3 vote).

roll_calls.note: LD number, date, tally need lookup
LD number, date, tally need lookup

rows_with_no_single_roll_call.name: 80% Net Operating Loss Limitation (TY2021 conformity)
rows_with_no_single_roll_call.note: A conformity mechanic (Maine adopting the federal TCJA 80% NOL limit for TY2021), not a standalone up/down vote. Most likely rode inside a 2021 conformity/budget act (candidate: PL 2021, c. 1 / LD 220, or the FY2022-23 budget). Confirm the vehicle before attributing; may have no clean member-level vote on this provision.

rows_with_no_single_roll_call.name: Hospital tax base year updated (repeated)
rows_with_no_single_roll_call.note: Not a single vote — the base year is refreshed in EVERY biennial/supplemental budget under Mills (c. 412, c. 643, c. 650, and earlier). Attribution, if any, is to whichever budget roll call. Treat as a recurring rider, not a discrete tax vote.

rows_with_no_single_roll_call.name: PaintCare stewardship fee increase
rows_with_no_single_roll_call.note: NOT a legislative vote. Approved administratively by the Maine DEP on a proposal from PaintCare (a nonprofit). No legislator voted on it — this row must show 'no roll call' for every member and should probably be excluded from the voting matrix entirely.

row_to_bill_index.30: none (DEP administrative — not a legislative vote)
